title: Repeatable Production Readiness Closure Learning

summary.next_recommended_cycle: Repeatable Article Upgrade Cycle Planning with explicit approval

learning_points_from_ag07q: Post-mutation audit must be separate from the apply stage.
Marker scope proves whether a controlled one-article apply stayed within boundary.
Backup integrity is the rollback foundation.
A static article change does not imply publishing approval or backend activation.
AG07Z can close the controlled chain only if AG07Q passes.
ag07z_learning_points: The AG07 chain is now repeatable because it progressed from boundary to candidate to plan to one-article apply to post-mutation audit to closure.
The safest future pattern is one target article per controlled apply until batch controls are separately designed.
A static article change should remain separate from publish-ready approval, production JSONL persistence, database/Supabase activation and backend activation.
Each future article upgrade must preserve backup-first and audit-after doctrine.
AG07Z closes the chain but does not authorize the next cycle.

repeatable_operating_doctrine.instruction: AG06 foundation evidence must remain closed before any future article upgrade cycle starts.
Each future cycle must define its allowed and blocked actions before tooling or mutation.
A production-packet candidate must exist before any static article apply.
A controlled mutation plan, approval checklist, backup plan and rollback plan must exist before apply.
Future controlled applies should mutate only one explicitly approved target article per apply stage.
The target article must be backed up before any static apply.
Every apply must be followed by a post-mutation audit before closure.

repeatable_operating_doctrine.rule: Static file change is not publishing approval
repeatable_operating_doctrine.instruction: A committed static file change does not activate publishing approval, backend, Auth, Supabase, API or database write.

repeatable_operating_doctrine.rule: References and visuals require their own gates
repeatable_operating_doctrine.instruction: Reference URL population, insertion, visual generation and image credit population must remain controlled by their own approved stages.
Each stage must record review, registry, schema, learning and preview evidence for future repetition.

future_safe_operating_rules.allowed: select one target article only after explicit approval
future_safe_operating_rules.blocked: multi-article apply without a separate batch approval and audit design

future_safe_operating_rules.allowed: one controlled static article mutation with backup
future_safe_operating_rules.blocked: unbounded public article mutation or silent file edits

future_safe_operating_rules.allowed: reference insertion only after candidate URLs are verified and approved
future_safe_operating_rules.blocked: invented links, broken links, parked links, spam links or unverified reference insertion

future_safe_operating_rules.allowed: visual insertion only with image credit, alt text and caption
future_safe_operating_rules.blocked: uncredited images, missing alt text, layout-breaking assets or undocumented generated visuals

future_safe_operating_rules.allowed: governance artifacts and approved static file commits
future_safe_operating_rules.blocked: production JSONL append, database write or Supabase write without separate activation approval
backend, API, Auth, Supabase, subscriber/admin/payment activation
closing an apply chain without post-mutation audit and rollback readiness
